title: Module Project: Start Volunteer Hub History
summary: Continue the parallel volunteer-hub repository through five cumulative steps to produce a trustworthy first snapshot on main with a repository-local identity and a clean working tree.

sketchBlocks.title: Prepare a trustworthy snapshot for volunteer-hub
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
This project guides you through turning an ordinary folder named volunteer-hub into a small Git repository with one clear initial commit. You'll act as a volunteer coordinator who wants a reliable local history for the project files (README.md, index.html, styles.css, and notes/schedule.txt). The five progressive steps are: inspect the folder, initialize on main, set a repository-only identity, stage only the authored files, and commit the snapshot named "Start volunteer hub".

Why this order matters: inspection before initialization prevents surprises; setting a local identity keeps commits attributable without changing your global Git configuration; staging lets you control exactly which files belong in the first snapshot; and a single, focused commit makes future history easier to read and revert if necessary. Each sketch below pairs with a hands-on Try It exercise that opens the volunteer-hub workspace and checks one specific repository transition.

### Project Brief



**Scenario:** A volunteer coordinator has an ordinary volunteer-hub folder with a README and planning notes. Build its first trustworthy local snapshot.

**Your role:** Act as the person preparing the project for reliable local change tracking.

**Workspace:** Use volunteer-hub for every step. This same repository continues in the Module 2 and Module 3 projects.

**Deliverable:** One repository on main with local identity, one commit named Start volunteer hub, the authored files tracked, and a clean working tree.

quizDraft.title: Inspect the Ordinary Folder
quizDraft.prompt:
Open the workspace and inspect the volunteer-hub folder. Confirm the folder exists and contains README.md, index.html, styles.css, and notes/schedule.txt before the repository is initialized. Type the shell commands you would use to show these files and press Enter for each command.

The terminal opens inside `volunteer-hub/` with the required starting milestone ready. Type each requested terminal command and press Enter.
quizDraft.hint: Read the Git task "Inspect the Ordinary Folder" and identify the repository state you need to create.

help.concept: This Git exercise checks the repository state produced by "Inspect the Ordinary Folder".
help.hint_1: Use git status before and after each small change so you can see what moved between the working tree, staging area, and history.
help.hint_2: Match the requested file names, branch names, and commit purpose before clicking Check Answer.

# Work in the terminal; the starting repository state is ready.
# Type each requested command and press Enter.
quizDraft.instructions:
Open the workspace and inspect the volunteer-hub folder. Confirm the folder exists and contains README.md, index.html, styles.css, and notes/schedule.txt before the repository is initialized. Type the shell commands you would use to show these files and press Enter for each command.

The terminal opens inside `volunteer-hub/` with the required starting milestone ready. Type each requested terminal command and press Enter.

starterFiles.content:
# Work in the terminal; the starting repository state is ready.
# Type each requested command and press Enter.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu
rm -rf -- "volunteer-hub"
mkdir -p -- "volunteer-hub"
printf '%s\n' 'Cumulative Git practice project' > "volunteer-hub/README.md"

quizDraft.title: Initialize the Repository on main
quizDraft.prompt:
Initialize the volunteer-hub folder as a Git repository whose initial branch is main. Move into volunteer-hub, initialize on main, and run a status command to show untracked files. Type each command and press Enter.

The terminal opens inside `volunteer-hub/` with the required starting milestone ready. Type each requested terminal command and press Enter.
quizDraft.hint: Read the Git task "Initialize the Repository on main" and identify the repository state you need to create.

help.hint_1: Use git status before and after each small change so you can see what moved between the working tree, staging area, and history.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu
rm -rf -- "volunteer-hub"
mkdir -p -- "volunteer-hub"
printf '%s\n' 'Cumulative Git practice project' > "volunteer-hub/README.md"
git -C "volunteer-hub" init -q -b main

quizDraft.title: Configure repository-local identity
quizDraft.prompt:
Inside the initialized volunteer-hub repository, configure the repository-local user.name and user.email to the required values: user.name = "ZoeSkoul Learner" and user.email = "learner@zoeskoul.local". Use git config --local and then show the configured values.

The terminal opens inside `volunteer-hub/` with the required starting milestone ready. Type each requested terminal command and press Enter.

quizDraft.title: Stage the First Snapshot
quizDraft.prompt:
Stage these four files into the index so they form a single proposed snapshot: README.md, index.html, styles.css, and notes/schedule.txt. Change into volunteer-hub, add only those files, and run git status --short to show they are staged. Type the commands and press Enter for each.

The terminal opens inside `volunteer-hub/` with the required starting milestone ready. Type each requested terminal command and press Enter.
quizDraft.hint: Read the Git task "Stage the First Snapshot" and identify the repository state you need to create.

help.concept: This Git exercise checks the repository state produced by "Stage the First Snapshot".
help.hint_1: Use git status before and after each small change so you can see what moved between the working tree, staging area, and history.
help.hint_2: Match the requested file names, branch names, and commit purpose before clicking Check Answer.

quizDraft.starterCode:
# Work in the terminal; the starting repository state is ready.
# Type each requested command and press Enter.
quizDraft.instructions:
Stage these four files into the index so they form a single proposed snapshot: README.md, index.html, styles.css, and notes/schedule.txt. Change into volunteer-hub, add only those files, and run git status --short to show they are staged. Type the commands and press Enter for each.

The terminal opens inside `volunteer-hub/` with the required starting milestone ready. Type each requested terminal command and press Enter.

starterFiles.content:
# Work in the terminal; the starting repository state is ready.
# Type each requested command and press Enter.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu
rm -rf -- "volunteer-hub"
mkdir -p -- "volunteer-hub"
printf '%s\n' 'Cumulative Git practice project' > "volunteer-hub/README.md"
git -C "volunteer-hub" init -q -b main
git -C "volunteer-hub" config user.name "ZoeSkoul Learner"
git -C "volunteer-hub" config user.email "learner@zoeskoul.local"
git -C "volunteer-hub" add -- "README.md"
git -C "volunteer-hub" commit -q -m "Start cumulative project"
printf '%s\n' 'Prepared practice change' >> "volunteer-hub/README.md"

quizDraft.prompt:
Commit the staged snapshot with the message "Start volunteer hub", then show the one-line log and confirm the working tree is clean. Type the commands needed and press Enter for each.

The terminal opens inside `volunteer-hub/` with the required starting milestone ready. Type each requested terminal command and press Enter.
